title: The Three Modes of Nature and Beyond
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “The Supreme Lord said: 'Oh best of persons, try to understand what I am about to say concerning the way someone is influenced by a certain mode of My material nature [*].” and develops toward “Know that one is wakeful in the mode of goodness, that one is sleepy in passion, that one is not aware in the ignorant mode of the living entity, and…”

This passage begins with “In the mode of goodness spiritual [Vedic] persons reach higher and higher, in the mode of ignorance one reaches head first lower and lower [births], and in the mode of passion one is stuck…” and develops toward “The living entity, who as an individual soul by Me thus was liberated from the basic qualities of nature that nestled in his mind, achieves thus, by…”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच गुणानाम् असम्मिश्राणां पुमान् येन यथा भवेत् । तन् मे पुरुष-वर्येदम् उपधारय शंसतः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca guṇānām asammiśrāṇāṃ pumān yena yathā bhavet / tan me puruṣa-varyedam upadhāraya śaṃsataḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Oh best of persons, try to understand what I am about to say concerning the way someone is influenced by a certain mode of My material nature [*].

verses.sanskrit: शमो दमस् तितिक्षेक्षा तपः सत्यं दया स्मृतिः । तुष्टिस् त्यागो ऽस्पृहा श्रद्धा ह्रीर् दयादिः स्व-निर्वृतिः
verses.transliteration: śamo damas titikṣekṣā tapaḥ satyaṃ dayā smṛtiḥ / tuṣṭis tyāgo 'spṛhā śraddhā hrīr dayādiḥ sva-nirvṛtiḥ
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: काम ईहा मदस् तृष्णा स्तम्भ आशीर् भिदा सुखम् । मदोत्साहो यशः-प्रीतिर् हास्यं वीर्यं बलोद्यमः
verses.transliteration: kāma īhā madas tṛṣṇā stambha āśīr bhidā sukham / madotsāho yaśaḥ-prītir hāsyaṃ vīryaṃ balodyamaḥ
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: क्रोधो लोभो ऽनृतं हिंसा याच्ञा दम्भः क्लमः कलिः । शोक-मोहौ विषादार्ती निद्राशा भीर् अनुद्यमः
verses.transliteration: krodho lobho 'nṛtaṃ hiṃsā yācñā dambhaḥ klamaḥ kaliḥ / śoka-mohau viṣādārtī nidrāśā bhīr anudyamaḥ
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वस्य रजसश् चैतास् तमसश् चानुपूर्वशः । वृत्तयो वर्णित-प्रायाः सन्निपातम् अथो शृणु
verses.transliteration: sattvasya rajasaś caitās tamasaś cānupūrvaśaḥ / vṛttayo varṇita-prāyāḥ sannipātam atho śṛṇu
verses.translation: With the basic quality of goodness one finds equanimity, sense control, tolerance, discrimination, penance, truthfulness, compassion, remembrance, contentment, renunciation, freedom from desire, faithfulness, modesty and pleasure within. With the mode of passion there is lust, endeavor, conceit, dissatisfaction, false pride, a desire for blessings, separatism, sense gratification, rashness, love of praise, ridicule, display of valor and hard sanctioning. With the natural quality of ignorance one runs into intolerance, greed, deceitfulness, violence, attention seeking, hypocrisy, listlessness, quarrel, lamentation, delusion, the suffering of depression, sloth, false expectations, fear and indolence. These, one after the other described by Me, constitute the majority of the effects of the modes. Hear now about their combinations [see also B.G. 14].

verses.sanskrit: सन्निपातस् त्व् अहम् इति ममेत्य् उद्धव या मतिः । व्यवहारः सन्निपातो मनो-मात्रेन्द्रियासुभिः
verses.transliteration: sannipātas tv aham iti mamety uddhava yā matiḥ / vyavahāraḥ sannipāto mano-mātrendriyāsubhiḥ
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, the notion of 'I am this way' and 'that is a trait of mine' that people have [in relation to these qualities] when they are engaged with their mind, senses, sense objects and life breath, reflects a combination of the modes [see also 11.23: 49, 11.24: 7, 11.24: 13].

verses.sanskrit: धर्मे चार्थे च कामे च यदासौ परिनिष्ठितः । गुणानां सन्निकर्षो ऽयं श्रद्धा-रति-धनावहः
verses.transliteration: dharme cārthe ca kāme ca yadāsau pariniṣṭhitaḥ / guṇānāṃ sannikarṣo 'yaṃ śraddhā-rati-dhanāvahaḥ
verses.translation: In case a person is fixed in his religiosity, economic development and sense gratification, [also] the resultant faith, wealth and enjoyment is an expression of the interaction of the different modes.

verses.sanskrit: प्रवृत्ति-लक्षणे निष्ठा पुमान् यर्हि गृहाश्रमे । स्व-धर्मे चानु तिष्ठेत गुणानां समितिर् हि सा
verses.transliteration: pravṛtti-lakṣaṇe niṣṭhā pumān yarhi gṛhāśrame / sva-dharme cānu tiṣṭheta guṇānāṃ samitir hi sā
verses.translation: When a person in family life is of a dedication characterized by sense gratification and thereafter performs his religious duties, a combination of the modes is a fact.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषं सत्त्व-संयुक्तम् अनुमीयाच् छमादिभिः । कामादिभी रजो-युक्तं क्रोधाद्यैस् तमसा युतम्
verses.transliteration: puruṣaṃ sattva-saṃyuktam anumīyāc chamādibhiḥ / kāmādibhī rajo-yuktaṃ krodhādyais tamasā yutam
verses.translation: From someone's self-control can be deduced that he is endowed with goodness and so on, his lust is indicative of the mode of passion and such, and from his anger etcetera one may conclude that he is caught in ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: यदा भजति मां भक्त्या निरपेक्षः स्व-कर्मभिः । तं सत्त्व-प्रकृतिं विद्यात् पुरुषं स्त्रियम् एव वा
verses.transliteration: yadā bhajati māṃ bhaktyā nirapekṣaḥ sva-karmabhiḥ / taṃ sattva-prakṛtiṃ vidyāt puruṣaṃ striyam eva vā
verses.translation: When someone worships Me with devotion and indifference about the results of his labor, such a person should be understood to be of a practice of goodness, whether he is a man or a woman.

verses.sanskrit: यदा आशिष आशास्य मां भजेत स्व-कर्मभिः । तं रजः-प्रकृतिं विद्यात् हिंसाम् आशास्य तामसम्
verses.transliteration: yadā āśiṣa āśāsya māṃ bhajeta sva-karmabhiḥ / taṃ rajaḥ-prakṛtiṃ vidyāt hiṃsām āśāsya tāmasam
verses.translation: When one in the fulfillment of one's duties worships Me hoping for benedictions, such devotion must be understood as being of the nature of passion, and when one does so with violent intentions one is of ignorance [see also B.G. 17: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस् तम इति गुणा जीवस्य नैव मे । चित्त-जा यैस् तु भूतानां सज्जमानो निबध्यते
verses.transliteration: sattvaṃ rajas tama iti guṇā jīvasya naiva me / citta-jā yais tu bhūtānāṃ sajjamāno nibadhyate
verses.translation: The modes of sattva, tamas and rajas influence the [conditioned] individual but not Me; one is bound to them because they manifest in the mind and lead to attachment to life-forms and sense-objects [see also B.G. 4: 14].

verses.sanskrit: यदेतरौ जयेत् सत्त्वं भास्वरं विशदं शिवम् । तदा सुखेन युज्येत धर्म-ज्ञानादिभिः पुमान्
verses.transliteration: yadetarau jayet sattvaṃ bhāsvaraṃ viśadaṃ śivam / tadā sukhena yujyeta dharma-jñānādibhiḥ pumān
verses.translation: When the mode of goodness - which is pure, luminous and auspicious - predominates over the other two [of passion and ignorance], a person will be blessed with happiness, religiosity, knowledge and other good qualities [see also B.G. 14: 11, 18: 37].

verses.sanskrit: यदा जयेत् तमः सत्त्वं रजः सङ्गं भिदा चलम् । तदा दुःखेन युज्येत कर्मणा यशसा श्रिया
verses.transliteration: yadā jayet tamaḥ sattvaṃ rajaḥ saṅgaṃ bhidā calam / tadā duḥkhena yujyeta karmaṇā yaśasā śriyā
verses.translation: When passion defeats goodness and ignorance one gets attached, wants to make a difference and tends to impermanence, because of which one becomes unhappy by profit minded actions and striving for a good name and wealth [see also B.G. 14: 12, 18: 38].

verses.sanskrit: यदा जयेद् रजः सत्त्वं तमो मूढं लयं जडम् । युज्येत शोक-मोहाभ्यां निद्रया हिंसयाशया
verses.transliteration: yadā jayed rajaḥ sattvaṃ tamo mūḍhaṃ layaṃ jaḍam / yujyeta śoka-mohābhyāṃ nidrayā hiṃsayāśayā
verses.translation: When ignorance dominates passion and goodness one's discrimination is defeated, one's consciousness is covered, one's initiative is lost and one becomes endowed with bewilderment, complaints, sleeping too much, violence and false hopes [see also B.G. 14: 13, 18: 39].

verses.sanskrit: यदा चित्तं प्रसीदेत इन्द्रियाणां च निर्वृतिः । देहे ऽभयं मनो-ऽसङ्गं तत् सत्त्वं विद्धि मत्-पदम्
verses.transliteration: yadā cittaṃ prasīdeta indriyāṇāṃ ca nirvṛtiḥ / dehe 'bhayaṃ mano-'saṅgaṃ tat sattvaṃ viddhi mat-padam
verses.translation: When one's consciousness clears up and the senses are not distracted, one achieves physical self-confidence and a detached mind; know that to be the goodness of My refuge.

verses.sanskrit: विकुर्वन् क्रियया चा-धीर् अनिवृत्तिश् च चेतसाम् । गात्रास्वास्थ्यं मनो भ्रान्तं रज एतैर् निशामय
verses.transliteration: vikurvan kriyayā cā-dhīr anivṛttiś ca cetasām / gātrāsvāsthyaṃ mano bhrāntaṃ raja etair niśāmaya
verses.translation: When the intelligence is disturbed by too much activity, when one fails to disengage from one's senses, when one is not at ease with one's body and when the mind is unsteady, you should understand that to be the symptoms of passion.

verses.sanskrit: सीदच् चित्तं विलीयेत चेतसो ग्रहणे ऽक्षमम् । मनो नष्टं तमो ग्लानिस् तमस् तद् उपधारय
verses.transliteration: sīdac cittaṃ vilīyeta cetaso grahaṇe 'kṣamam / mano naṣṭaṃ tamo glānis tamas tad upadhāraya
verses.translation: Failing in the higher functions of consciousness, getting dull, being unable to focus, not being mindful, not understanding things and being gloomy, you should recognize as the mode of ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: एधमाने गुणे सत्त्वे देवानां बलम् एधते । असुराणां च रजसि तमस्य् उद्धव रक्षसाम्
verses.transliteration: edhamāne guṇe sattve devānāṃ balam edhate / asurāṇāṃ ca rajasi tamasy uddhava rakṣasām
verses.translation: When goodness increases the strength of the gods increases, when passion increases the demons grow stronger, and when ignorance increases, Uddhava, the wild men will get on top.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वाज् जागरणं विद्याद् रजसा स्वप्नम् आदिशेत् । प्रस्वापं तमसा जन्तोस् तुरीयं त्रिषु सन्ततम्
verses.transliteration: sattvāj jāgaraṇaṃ vidyād rajasā svapnam ādiśet / prasvāpaṃ tamasā jantos turīyaṃ triṣu santatam
verses.translation: Know that one is wakeful in the mode of goodness, that one is sleepy in passion, that one is not aware in the ignorant mode of the living entity, and that the fourth [transcendental] state [of consciousness turya] pervades the three [see also 7.7: 25 and B.G. 6: 16].

verses.sanskrit: उपर्य् उपरि गच्छन्ति सत्त्वेन ब्राह्मणा जनाः । तमसाधो ऽध आ-मुख्याद् रजसान्तर-चारिणः
verses.transliteration: upary upari gacchanti sattvena brāhmaṇā janāḥ / tamasādho 'dha ā-mukhyād rajasāntara-cāriṇaḥ
verses.translation: In the mode of goodness spiritual [Vedic] persons reach higher and higher, in the mode of ignorance one reaches head first lower and lower [births], and in the mode of passion one is stuck in between [in attachments, see also B.G. 6: 45, 16: 19].

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वे प्रलीनाः स्वर् यान्ति नर-लोकं रजो-लयाः । तमो-लयास् तु निरयं यान्ति माम् एव निर्गुणाः
verses.transliteration: sattve pralīnāḥ svar yānti nara-lokaṃ rajo-layāḥ / tamo-layās tu nirayaṃ yānti mām eva nirguṇāḥ
verses.translation: Those who die in goodness go to heaven, those who die in passion go to the human world and those who die in ignorance go to hell. They however who are free from the modes come to Me [see also B.G. 9: 25, 14: 18].

verses.sanskrit: मद्-अर्पणं निष्फलं वा सात्त्विकं निज-कर्म तत् । राजसं फल-सङ्कल्पं हिंसा-प्रायादि तामसम्
verses.transliteration: mad-arpaṇaṃ niṣphalaṃ vā sāttvikaṃ nija-karma tat / rājasaṃ phala-saṅkalpaṃ hiṃsā-prāyādi tāmasam
verses.translation: Work dutifully done as a sacrifice unto Me without desiring the fruits is in the mode of goodness, work done with a profit motive is of the mode of passion and work performed with violence and pressure and such, is of the mode of ignorance [B.G. 17: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: कैवल्यं सात्त्विकं ज्ञानं रजो वैकल्पिकं च यत् । प्राकृतं तामसं ज्ञानं मन्-निष्ठं निर्गुणं स्मृतम्
verses.transliteration: kaivalyaṃ sāttvikaṃ jñānaṃ rajo vaikalpikaṃ ca yat / prākṛtaṃ tāmasaṃ jñānaṃ man-niṣṭhaṃ nirguṇaṃ smṛtam
verses.translation: Spiritual knowledge of detachment is of the mode of goodness, fostering opinions one is of the mode of passion, and a materialistic conviction belongs to the mode of ignorance. Spiritual knowledge focussed on Me [however] is considered to be free from the modes [see also 6.14: 2].

verses.sanskrit: वनं तु सात्त्विको वासो ग्रामो राजस उच्यते । तामसं द्यूत-सदनं मन्-निकेतं तु निर्गुणम्
verses.transliteration: vanaṃ tu sāttviko vāso grāmo rājasa ucyate / tāmasaṃ dyūta-sadanaṃ man-niketaṃ tu nirguṇam
verses.translation: To have one's residence in the forest is of the mode of goodness, to reside in a town is said to be of the mode of passion, to reside in a gambling house is of the mode of ignorance, but My residence is elevated above the modes [see also 7.12: 22, 11.18: 25].

verses.sanskrit: सात्त्विकः कारको ऽसङ्गी रागान्धो राजसः स्मृतः । तामसः स्मृति-विभ्रष्टो निर्गुणो मद्-अपाश्रयः
verses.transliteration: sāttvikaḥ kārako 'saṅgī rāgāndho rājasaḥ smṛtaḥ / tāmasaḥ smṛti-vibhraṣṭo nirguṇo mad-apāśrayaḥ
verses.translation: A worker free from attachment is of the mode of goodness, blinded by personal desire one is of the mode of passion, having lost one's memory one speaks of the mode of ignorance [see 11.22: 38-39], [but] the one who has taken shelter of Me is free from the modes.

verses.sanskrit: सात्त्विक्य् आध्यात्मिकी श्रद्धा कर्म-श्रद्धा तु राजसी । तामस्य् अधर्मे या श्रद्धा मत्-सेवायां तु निर्गुणा
verses.transliteration: sāttviky ādhyātmikī śraddhā karma-śraddhā tu rājasī / tāmasy adharme yā śraddhā mat-sevāyāṃ tu nirguṇā
verses.translation: In the mode of goodness one believes in spiritual matters, in the mode of passion one believes in fruitful activities, in the mode of ignorance one is irreligious, but one is transcendental to the modes with faith in My devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: पथ्यं पूतम् अनायस्तम् आहार्यं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् । राजसं चेन्द्रिय-प्रेष्ठं तामसं चार्ति-दाशुचि
verses.transliteration: pathyaṃ pūtam anāyastam āhāryaṃ sāttvikaṃ smṛtam / rājasaṃ cendriya-preṣṭhaṃ tāmasaṃ cārti-dāśuci
verses.translation: Food that is wholesome, pure and attained effortlessly is considered to be of the mode of goodness, [strongly] catering to the senses it is of the mode of passion, and impure food that makes one suffer is of ignorance [see also B.G. 17: 7-10].

verses.sanskrit: सात्त्विकं सुखम् आत्मोत्थं विषयोत्थं तु राजसम् । तामसं मोह-दैन्योत्थं निर्गुणं मद्-अपाश्रयम्
verses.transliteration: sāttvikaṃ sukham ātmotthaṃ viṣayotthaṃ tu rājasam / tāmasaṃ moha-dainyotthaṃ nirguṇaṃ mad-apāśrayam
verses.translation: Happiness derived from the soul is of the mode of goodness, generated by sense objects it is of passion, happiness derived from delusion and depravity is of the mode of ignorance, but free from the modes happiness is found in Me [see 11.15: 17 & B.G. 5: 21, but also 6: 7].

verses.sanskrit: द्रव्यं देशः फलं कालो ज्ञानं कर्म च कारकः । श्रद्धावस्थाकृतिर् निष्ठा त्रै-गुण्यः सर्व एव हि
verses.transliteration: dravyaṃ deśaḥ phalaṃ kālo jñānaṃ karma ca kārakaḥ / śraddhāvasthākṛtir niṣṭhā trai-guṇyaḥ sarva eva hi
verses.translation: Material substance, the place, the fruit of action, time, knowledge, activity, the performer, faith, the state of consciousness, and the species and destinations of life thus all belong to the three gunas.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे गुण-मया भावाः पुरुषाव्यक्त-धिष्ठिताः । दृष्टं श्रुतं अनुध्यातं बुद्ध्या वा पुरुषर्षभ
verses.transliteration: sarve guṇa-mayā bhāvāḥ puruṣāvyakta-dhiṣṭhitāḥ / dṛṣṭaṃ śrutaṃ anudhyātaṃ buddhyā vā puruṣarṣabha
verses.translation: Oh best among men, all that exists, being seen, heard or pictured in one's mind, is a composition of the three basic qualities that was established by the unseen [Original] Enjoyer.

verses.sanskrit: एताः संसृतयः पुंसो गुण-कर्म-निबन्धनाः । येनेमे निर्जिताः सौम्य गुणा जीवेन चित्त-जाः । भक्ति-योगेन मन्-निष्ठो मद्-भावाय प्रपद्यते
verses.transliteration: etāḥ saṃsṛtayaḥ puṃso guṇa-karma-nibandhanāḥ / yeneme nirjitāḥ saumya guṇā jīvena citta-jāḥ / bhakti-yogena man-niṣṭho mad-bhāvāya prapadyate
verses.translation: These forms of existence [and stages of life] of the [repeatedly incarnating] living being, are bound to the operation of the modes. Oh gentle one, the individual soul who, dedicated to Me in bhakti-yoga, conquers these natural qualities that manifest themselves in the mind, qualifies for My transcendental love.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् देहम् इमं लब्ध्वा ज्ञान-विज्ञान-सम्भवम् । गुण-सङ्गं विनिर्धूय मां भजन्तु विचक्षणाः
verses.transliteration: tasmād deham imaṃ labdhvā jñāna-vijñāna-sambhavam / guṇa-saṅgaṃ vinirdhūya māṃ bhajantu vicakṣaṇāḥ
verses.translation: They who obtained this human body by which one acquires knowledge and develops wisdom, should therefore be as smart to shake off their attachment to the natural modes and worship Me.

verses.sanskrit: निःसङ्गो मां भजेद् विद्वान् अप्रमत्तो जितेन्द्रियः । रजस् तमश् चाभिजयेत् सत्त्व-संसेवया मुनिः
verses.transliteration: niḥsaṅgo māṃ bhajed vidvān apramatto jitendriyaḥ / rajas tamaś cābhijayet sattva-saṃsevayā muniḥ
verses.translation: A learned man should worship Me free from attachment; attentively having subdued his senses a sage should take to the mode of goodness and conquer the modes of passion and ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं चाभिजयेद् युक्तो नैरपेक्ष्येण शान्त-धीः । सम्पद्यते गुणैर् मुक्तो जीवो जीवं विहाय माम्
verses.transliteration: sattvaṃ cābhijayed yukto nairapekṣyeṇa śānta-dhīḥ / sampadyate guṇair mukto jīvo jīvaṃ vihāya mām
verses.translation: With his intelligence pacified he, being connected [in bhakti] without any [other] dependency, should also conquer the mode of goodness. The embodied soul who [thus] freed from the modes gives up the cause of his conditioning, reaches Me.

verses.sanskrit: जीवो जीव-विनिर्मुक्तो गुणैश् चाशय-सम्भवैः । मयैव ब्रह्मणा पूर्णो न बहिर् नान्तरश् चरेत्
verses.transliteration: jīvo jīva-vinirmukto guṇaiś cāśaya-sambhavaiḥ / mayaiva brahmaṇā pūrṇo na bahir nāntaraś caret
verses.translation: The living entity, who as an individual soul by Me thus was liberated from the basic qualities of nature that nestled in his mind, achieves thus, by dint of the Absolute Truth, complete fulfillment and will neither internally nor externally, any longer wander around.'
